pages.name: Home
pages.title: Welcome to 101 Cast Stone
content:
101 Cast Stone specializes in the production of high-quality, custom cast stone products that serve as architectural features, trim, ornamentation, and facing for buildings. We also manufacture a full line of fireplace surrounds and mantels in a wide variety of styles to match any decor. We also specialize in Stone Masonry including both natural stone and stone veneer.

​

Cast stone serves as an appealing, durable and cost-effective alternative to natural cut limestone, brownstone, sandstone, bluestone, granite, slate, keystone, travertine, and other natural building stones. With our wide and varied selection of architectural styles and finishes, 101 Cast Stone products provide the perfect choice of just the right elements to enhance the style and beauty of your home.

101 Cast Stone specializes in the production of high-quality, custom cast stone products that serve as architectural features, trim, ornamentation, and facing for buildings. We also manufacture a full line of fireplace surrounds and mantels in a wide variety of styles to match any decor. We also specialize in Stone Masonry including both natural stone and stone veneer.

Cast stone serves as an appealing, durable and cost-effective alternative to natural cut limestone, brownstone, sandstone, bluestone, granite, slate, keystone, travertine, and other natural building stones. With our wide and varied selection of architectural styles and finishes, 101 Cast Stone products provide the perfect choice of just the right elements to enhance the style and beauty of your home.
Call us today at (805) 460-6060 to discuss your unique project requirements.

pages.name: Products
pages.title: Our Products
content:
Our Products

101 Cast Stone specializes in the production of cast stone, an excellent masonry product that can be used as an architectural feature, trim, ornament, or facing for buildings. We manufacture a full line of architectural products in both standard and custom styles and dimensions to suit your specific project needs. All of our cast stone products are offered in a choice of seven colors and three texture finishes.

Contact us to discuss our custom solutions to your specific requirements.

​

Cast stone serves as an appealing and durable alternative to natural cut limestone, brownstone, sandstone, bluestone, granite, slate, keystone, travertine, and other natural building stones. As a result of our attention to detail and our finishing techniques, in many cases our cast stone products are nearly indistinguishable from real stone, as in the fountain photo on the right which is made of cast stone.

​

We also specialize in Stone Masonry and can help you transform the interior or exterior of your home with the look and feel of stone. Whether it is natural stone or stone veneer, we can add a natural and sophisticated look to your space. Check out the Stone Masonry section of our site to see some of our past projects.

​

101 Cast Stone will work with you to implement the fulfillment of your individual and unique project – call us at (805) 460-6060 to discuss your specific requirements.

101 Cast Stone will work with you to implement the fulfillment of your individual and unique project – call us at (805) 460-6060 to discuss your specific requirements.

pages.name: About Us
pages.title: ABOUT US | 101 Cast Stone
content:
About 101 Cast Stone

​

       Our owner, Clark Baird, began his career in the industy working in a family-owned precast design and manufacturing business in Southern California named Sierra Concrete Design. Over the years, his role in the company grew and after moving to the Central Coast he became Area Sales Director for multiple regions of California. At that point, in part due to his efforts, the business was yielding upwards of 10 million dollars in sales.

 

       Clark Baird started 101 Cast Stone in the summer of 2006. After moving to Atascadero, he realized the need for a high-end cast stone facility on the central coast.

Today, 101 Cast Stone is one of the premier designers of cast stone products in California. With his staff of over 15 full and part-time employees, they recognize the following:

A person's home is their castle and stands as a testament to their elegance and personality.

Similar to jewelry accessorizing the one you love; your home's facade is glamourized by a sophisticated arbor or decadent backyard.

Few items will increase the stylishness of your castle like stone masonry or cast stone accents.

101 Cast Stone continues to work on a wide array of projects for developers, building contractors and residential customers.

Our mission and our passion is to continue to provide our customers with excellent products & service while helping them to accomplish their home or business beautification projects.

​

​

Here's just a few of our past and current customers:

 

American Premier Homes

Daou Winery

Midland Pacific Builders

Barefoot Pools

Embers Fireplaces & Grills

Nostalgic's Inc

City of Atascadero

Fordens

Paso Robles Community Church

City of Paso Robles

Gary Kramer Guitar Cellars

Shea Homes

Coastal Community Builders

Halsell Builders

Stalwork Construction

content:
Cast Stone Colors & Finishes
​

Our cast stone products can be produced in various stone colors and texture finishes to match your house and decor. Shown below are our standard colors and finishes. We can also product custom colors and texture finishes – call us at (805) 460-6060 to discuss your specific requirements.
 

Cast Stone Colors

pages.name: Design & Manufacturing Process
pages.title: Design & Manufacturing Process
content:
Design & Manufacturing Process
​

1. The 101 Cast Stone Professional Design Team has an extensive offering of Cast Stone Products, Fireplaces & Mantels, and Stone Masonry. If you do not see something that commands your attention here, we can create a custom configuration utilizing our current stock or generate something completely new. With our designers the possibilities are limitless.

​

2. Our Sales & Design professionals will work with you to determine the Cast Stone, Fireplace or Stone Masonry project requirements and items that you are looking for.

​

3. After the design of your architectural items and/or fireplace system is completed and confirmed by you, our team begins the process of creating the items.

​

4. If needed, any molds that may be required to be created or modified are processed in our Custom Mold Shop (see below). We then prepare the molds for production.  

​

​

​

​

​

​

​

​

​

​

​

​

​

​

​

​

​

​

5. When the molds associated with your project are properly prepared for casting, they are delivered to the Manufacturing floor.

​

​

​

​

​

​

​

​

​

 

 

 

6. The cast stone material is mixed in the color you’ve specified and is packed by hand into the mold in a specific fashion to achieve the desired finish. Following the cast stone, a very specific mix of fiberglass materials is then packed by hand into the mold. The molds are then left to dry for approximately one day depending on the particular detail.

​

​

​

​

​

​

 

 

 

7. After curing for the necessary amount of time, the pieces are removed from the molds and checked by Quality Control to make sure they pass inspection. They are then stored in the appropriate staging location while we finish compiling all the items for your order.

​

8. Once the order is finished being made, we will schedule with the customer to deliver and install the items and provide you with the beautiful cast stone elements to beautify and enhance your home.

1. The 101 Cast Stone Professional Design Team has an extensive offering of Cast Stone Products, Fireplaces & Mantels, and Stone Masonry. If you do not see something that commands your attention here, we can create a custom configuration utilizing our current stock or generate something completely new. With our designers the possibilities are limitless.
2. Our Sales & Design professionals will work with you to determine the Cast Stone, Fireplace or Stone Masonry project requirements and items that you are looking for.
3. After the design of your architectural items and/or fireplace system is completed and confirmed by you, our team begins the process of creating the items.
4. If needed, any molds that may be required to be created or modified are processed in our Custom Mold Shop (see below). We then prepare the molds for production.
5. When the molds associated with your project are properly prepared for casting, they are delivered to the Manufacturing floor.

6. The cast stone material is mixed in the color you’ve specified and is packed by hand into the mold in a specific fashion to achieve the desired finish. Following the cast stone, a very specific mix of fiberglass materials is then packed by hand into the mold. The molds are then left to dry for approximately one day depending on the particular detail.

7. After curing for the necessary amount of time, the pieces are removed from the molds and checked by Quality Control to make sure they pass inspection. They are then stored in the appropriate staging location while we finish compiling all the items for your order.
8. Once the order is finished being made, we will schedule with the customer to deliver and install the items and provide you with the beautiful cast stone elements to beautify and enhance your home.

pages.name: FAQ
pages.title: Frequently Asked Questions
content:
Frequently Asked Questions
​

Here are the answers to common questions that we receive about our cast stone products. Please call us at (805) 460-6060 if you have other questions, or if you wish to discuss your unique project requirements.

Here are the answers to common questions that we receive about our cast stone products. Please call us at (805) 460-6060 if you have other questions, or if you wish to discuss your unique project requirements.
How do I order a Fireplace Mantel?

Contact us via phone, email or the contact form on this website,
(Click Here) and we will walk you through the entire exciting process of creating the fireplace products of your choice.
How do I measure my Fireplace?

If you are local to our area of California, our sales staff will schedule an appointment to visit your residence or jobsite to verify the measurements. If you are out of the area, please download our Fireplace Surround Worksheet located under the Technical Info section of our website. Once you’ve taken the required measurements, our sales staff will review your worksheet with you to confirm accuracy.

All of our fireplace products are quoted with installation by our team of professionals. However, if you would like to complete the installation yourself, many of our products may be installed by others as long as our installation requirements are adhered to.

No. Our cast stone products are available in over 7 standard colors and 3 different finishes. However if you wish to have an artist antique or faux finish the mantel, our products will accept their materials.
How long will Cast Stone last?
Cast Stone has been a prime building material for hundreds of years with a very long life span. The earliest known use of cast stone dates to around 1138 A.D. in Carcassonne, France.
Do I need to seal the Fireplace Mantel?

Sealing is recommended but not required. Cast Stone products are porous and therefore subject to staining. We can provide you with the appropriate sealer at the time of installation or can arrange to seal the package after the product has cured.
How do I clean my Cast Stone?
With lukewarm water and a clean white cloth.
What is the Fireplace Mantel made of?

Everything we make is made of modern-day Cast Stone, a process that has been in use for over 70 years. Included in the mix are Portland Cement, Crushed Quartz, Color Pigment, Fiberglass Reinforcing Materials and Polymer. This labor intensive process results in a finished product that is much stronger and similar in appearance to natural stone. It is also much cheaper than natural stone. Cast Stone is a non-combustible product and can be used directly next to the firebox opening. This eliminates the need and cost of other interior surrounds that are required with a wood or plaster mantel.
Definitely! If you are unable to find a style you are looking for in our standard product line, we will be happy to work with you and explore other possible options.
Do you do demolition of existing fireplace?
Are there different colors & finishes available?
Yes. We have a number of standard colors and different finish styles available to choose from. We can also create custom colors if needed.
What if the firebox opening does not seem to fit the standard mantel dimensions?

If the fireplace mantel you desire does not appear to fit your firebox, we can work with our design team to come up with the best solution to alter the mantel in such a manner that will retain the integrity of the design.
What is the lead time for my mantel?

Our cast stone items are a custom product made by hand. With that in mind, please allow 2 to 3 weeks for production and installation of your fireplace mantel. If you require a different time frame, please let us know and we will do whatever we can to accommodate you.
How is the Fireplace Mantel delivered?
Our crew will deliver the mantel at the time of installation. If installation is not being provided, alternate delivery options are available.

Still have a question?
